# Day 1 Setup Guide

Step-by-step terminal commands to go from zero to first GitHub push.

---

## Step 1: Create GitHub repo

1. Go to github.com → New repository
2. Name: `ProteinLens`
3. Description: `Real-time on-device food classification iOS app with protein estimation using CoreML`
4. Set to **Public**
5. Do NOT initialise with README (we'll push our own)
6. Click **Create repository**

---

## Step 2: Clone and set up locally

```bash
# Clone your new empty repo
git clone https://github.com/bhatt-aditya03/ProteinLens.git
cd ProteinLens

# Copy all generated files into this folder
# (README.md, .gitignore, ml/, assets/ folders)
```

---

## Step 3: Python environment

```bash
# Create a virtual environment
python3 -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate        # macOS/Linux
# venv\Scripts\activate         # Windows

# Install dependencies
pip install -r ml/requirements.txt

# Verify TensorFlow installed
python -c "import tensorflow as tf; print(tf.__version__)"
```

---

## Step 4: Download Food-101 dataset

**Option A — Google Colab (recommended, free GPU):**
1. Upload `ml/day1_eda.ipynb` to [colab.research.google.com](https://colab.research.google.com)
2. Runtime → Change runtime type → **GPU**
3. Uncomment the `tensorflow_datasets` cell and run

**Option B — Kaggle:**
```bash
pip install kaggle
# Place your kaggle.json in ~/.kaggle/
mkdir -p ml/data
kaggle datasets download -d dansbecker/food-101 --unzip -p ml/data/
```

---

## Step 5: Run the EDA notebook

```bash
cd ml
jupyter notebook day1_eda.ipynb
# Run all cells — charts save to assets/
```

---

## Step 6: First GitHub push

```bash
cd ..   # back to ProteinLens root

git add .
git commit -m "Day 1: Repo setup, Food-101 EDA, protein database (20 classes)"
git push origin main
```

---

## Expected after Day 1

Your repo should contain:
```
ProteinLens/
├── README.md
├── .gitignore
├── ml/
│   ├── requirements.txt
│   ├── protein_data.json
│   └── day1_eda.ipynb
└── assets/
    ├── eda_protein_chart.png   (generated by notebook)
    └── eda_samples.png         (generated by notebook, needs data)
```
